title: Stale-while-revalidate state
prompt_md:
Implement `stale_while_revalidate(entry, now)` returning one of the strings `"fresh"`, `"stale"`, or `"expired"`.

An entry has `fresh_until` and `hard_until` timestamps (`fresh_until <= hard_until`).

- `"fresh"` if `now < entry["fresh_until"]` — serve directly.
- `"stale"` if `fresh_until <= now < entry["hard_until"]` — serve the old value but trigger a background refresh.
- `"expired"` otherwise (`now >= hard_until`) — must refetch before serving.
hints: Check the fresh window first, then the stale window, then fall through to expired.
All boundaries: fresh_until is exclusive on the upper side (now < fresh_until is fresh); hard_until is inclusive for expired (now >= hard_until).
